name: Froggatt-Nielsen mechanism
summary: An abelian U(1)_FN flavor symmetry under which each fermion has an integer charge q_i. Yukawa couplings forbidden by the symmetry are generated by insertions of a flavon scalar that picks up a small VEV, so y_ij ~ epsilon^{q_i + q_j} where epsilon = <flavon> / Lambda_FN. The hierarchical fermion masses and CKM mixing pattern then follow from a small set of integer charges and one expansion parameter.

addresses_puzzles.note: Six orders of magnitude in quark masses compress to one continuous parameter (epsilon ~ Cabibbo angle) plus a set of integer charges. Charged-lepton hierarchy compresses similarly.
Wolfenstein lambda is identified with epsilon; the powers of lambda in V_us, V_cb, V_ub follow from the same integer-charge assignments.

touches_benchmarks.note: FN with epsilon ~ Cabibbo angle and integer charges is the canonical mechanism that produces the compression ansatz this benchmark codifies.
Wolfenstein lambda is identified with the FN expansion parameter epsilon; lambda^n powers in V_CKM follow from charge assignments.
FN-induced flavor-violating couplings can produce K-Kbar and D-Dbar mixing at observable levels; consistency with measured mixing is a non-trivial constraint on the charge assignments.

introduces.note: Continuous parameters: epsilon and Lambda_FN. Integer charges are not free continuous parameters but are chosen to reproduce the data; counting them as zero is conservative for compression purposes since they are highly constrained by the requirement to reproduce nine masses + CKM with one epsilon.

history: Froggatt and Nielsen (1979). Modular flavor symmetries (Feruglio 2017) re-derive the structure from a different mathematical starting point. Variants with multiple U(1)s, non-abelian families (A_4, S_4), and discrete subgroups of SU(3) flavor are extensively studied.

references.citation: Froggatt and Nielsen, 'Hierarchy of quark masses, Cabibbo angles and CP violation', Nucl. Phys. B 147, 277 (1979)
Feruglio, 'Are neutrino masses modular forms?', in From My Vast Repertoire (2019)
